steps.title: Set the curves equal and rearrange
steps.content:
The system intersects where $x - c = -4(x - 8)^2$. Expand and rearrange to standard form:
$$4x^2 - 63x + (256 - c) = 0$$
The number of real solutions equals the number of intersections.

steps.title: Apply the strict discriminant condition
steps.content:
For two distinct real solutions, the discriminant must be strictly positive ($\Delta > 0$):
$$(-63)^2 - 4(4)(256 - c) > 0$$
$$3969 - 4096 + 16c > 0$$
$$c > \frac{127}{16} \approx 7.94$$

steps.title: Pick the choice satisfying the strict bound
steps.content: Choice C ($c = \frac{127}{16}$) gives discriminant exactly $0$, producing a single tangent point — not two distinct solutions. Choice D ($c = 13$) satisfies $c > \frac{127}{16}$ since $13 > 7.94$, so it produces the required two distinct solutions. That matches choice D — D is correct.
